title: rosa communis var. tardata
intro: Rosa communis var. tardata, also known as the Late Dog Rose, is a variety of the Rosa communis species, a wild rose native to Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. tardata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Rosa communis var. tardata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of rose petal jam and other medicinal products.
